name: Restore the accepted-correct hot-band surface and apply a warp-local B XOR/interleaved consumer swizzle with zero extra shared footprint

summary.name: Restore the accepted-correct hot-band surface and apply a warp-local B XOR/interleaved consumer swizzle with zero extra shared footprint
summary.hypothesis: Round 12 showed that spending extra shared-memory budget on a deeper pipeline did not buy real hot-band speed: the main 256x128 kernel moved only from about 41.11 ms to 41.18 ms while barrier stalls jumped from about 6.59% to 20.24%, and correctness broke on top. That weakens the stage family for now. The user's remaining strongest untried constraint set is consumer-side B feed repair with three hard gates: no extra shared tile, no extra CTA barrier, no reduction in the steady-state pipeline. The current hot-band kernel still uses only a simple warp-group skew on B shared storage. A light XOR or interleaved remap in the logical B shared column mapping is the best next shot because it targets operand delivery directly while honoring those gates.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Shared-memory bank behavior and warp-local B operand delivery in the hot-band PTX consumer path.
summary.risk: High. A broken remap silently corrupts results, and even a correct remap can worsen global-to-shared coalescing or register use enough to erase any bank benefit.
summary.stop_condition: Reject immediately if correctness fails, shared memory per block increases, or hot-band time does not beat the restored accepted surface.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
